symbols: cross — the instrument of salvation, where divine love meets human sin
INRI inscription — Jesus of Nazareth, King of the Jews, the ironic proclamation of Christ's true kingship
Mary standing at the cross — the Mother of Sorrows whose suffering unites her to her Son's sacrifice
Mary Magdalene kneeling — the penitent disciple clinging to the cross in devotion
John the Beloved standing — the faithful disciple to whom Christ entrusts His mother
description: A dark crucifixion scene within a gilded frame. Christ hangs on a wooden cross at center, wearing a crown of thorns and a white loincloth, with the INRI plaque above. At the left, a veiled woman in dark robes stands with hands clasped in grief. At the right, a younger figure in a green garment stands with one hand raised. At the foot of the cross, a figure in a red-brown garment kneels, embracing the base. The background is very dark, focusing all attention on the illuminated figures.
theological_description: The Crucifixion is the central event of Christian faith — the moment where God's justice and mercy meet in the atoning sacrifice of His Son (Romans 3:25). The three figures at the cross represent the faithful remnant: Mary the Mother, John the Beloved Disciple, and Mary Magdalene the penitent. John's Gospel records that from the cross, Jesus entrusted Mary to John and John to Mary (John 19:26-27), creating a new family born not of blood but of grace. Mary's presence at the cross fulfills Simeon's prophecy that a sword would pierce her soul (Luke 2:35). Mary Magdalene, from whom seven demons were cast out, clings to the cross as the place where her redemption was purchased. Together they embody the Church at the foot of the cross: sorrowful yet faithful, witnessing the death that conquers death. The darkness that surrounds the scene reflects the cosmic darkness that covered the land (Matthew 27:45).

scripture_refs.reason: Mary, John, and the women at the foot of the cross — the exact scene depicted
The crucifixion of Jesus with darkness covering the land
The sword piercing Mary's soul, fulfilled as she witnesses her Son's death
God presented Christ as a propitiation through His blood — the theological meaning of the cross
The Son of God who loved me and gave Himself for me — the personal dimension of the cross
